title: gh-inbox: CLI tool that surfaces GitHub items needing your attention with smart CODEOWNERS filtering
V0.2: gh inbox prs --authored with unresolved thread detection
Filter modes overlap: direct shows codeowner PRs and vice versa
description:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `gh inbox prs review` (unfiltered) — PR #118681 shows SOURCE=codeowner
2. Run `gh inbox prs review --filter=direct` — same PR appears in results
3. Run `gh inbox prs review --filter=codeowner` — same PR also appears

PR #118681 appears in **both** filtered views.

## Expected Behavior

- `--filter=direct`: Show only PRs where I am **explicitly** tagged as reviewer (not via CODEOWNERS). The SOURCE column should show `direct` for all results.
- `--filter=codeowner`: Show only PRs where I am assigned **because of CODEOWNERS**. The SOURCE column should show `codeowner` for all results.
- The two filters should be **mutually exclusive** — a PR appears in one or the other, never both.

## Actual Behavior

`FilterDirect()` in `internal/filter/filter.go` includes sole-codeowner PRs as a fallback:

```go
// FilterDirect includes a PR when:
// - at least one of my review requests is NOT codeowner, OR
// - ALL mine are codeowner but I'm the sole reviewer (no others)
if allTrue(rc.mineCodeOwner) && rc.otherCount > 0 {
    continue  // skip only if others exist
}
```

This means when the user is the sole reviewer via CODEOWNERS, the PR appears in both `--filter=direct` and `--filter=codeowner`.

## Root Cause

The current `FilterDirect` logic conflates "sole reviewer" with "directly assigned". The intent was to avoid hiding PRs where no one else would review, but this breaks the semantic meaning of the filter modes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--filter=direct` shows only PRs with at least one non-CODEOWNERS review request for me
- [ ] `--filter=codeowner` shows only PRs where ALL my review requests are from CODEOWNERS
- [ ] The two filters are mutually exclusive (no overlap)
- [ ] Unfiltered (`--filter=all`) continues to show everything
- [ ] Update tests in `internal/filter/filter_test.go` to reflect new semantics
- [ ] SOURCE column in table output is consistent with filter results
close_reason: Implemented clean boolean partition: FilterDirect uses !allTrue, FilterCodeowner uses allTrue. Fixed tests 3 and 7, added mutual exclusivity tests 12-17.

title: Rework filter modes: direct/team/codeowner semantics
description:
Redesign the 3 filter modes so they cleanly partition review requests:

- **direct**: I am in reviewRequests AND (`!asCodeOwner` OR no other reviewers on the PR)
- **team**: my team is requested (unchanged)
- **codeowner**: fallback — `!direct && !team`

Research report: docs/research/2026-02-26-github-api-codeowners-vs-manual-reviewers.md

## Acceptance Criteria
- filterDirect uses new predicate: explicit request OR sole reviewer
- filterCodeowner is a fallback (not direct, not team)
- filterTeam is unchanged
- make test passes
- All existing TestFilterCodeowner cases updated to reflect new semantics

title: T3: Team cache + CODEOWNERS filtering (filtered JSON)
description: Create internal/github/team_members.go (lazy REST cache, fail-open), internal/filter/codeowners.go (pure filter logic, 3 modes: default/include-all/solo). Wire into cmd/prs.go. 7+ table-driven test scenarios.

title: V0.1: gh inbox prs --review with CODEOWNERS filtering
Research: GitHub API CODEOWNERS vs manual reviewer distinction
description:
Research whether GitHub API can distinguish between auto-assigned reviewers (CODEOWNERS) and manually requested reviewers on pull requests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Research completed with confidence >80%
- Report saved to durable location
- Session artifacts preserved in checkpoint directory
close_reason: Research completed. Report saved to docs/research/. Feature inbox-cbg created for follow-up work.

title: Update test cases for reworked filter modes
description:
Update filter_test.go to match the new semantics for all 3 modes.

## TestFilterDirect changes

Add:
- `"sole reviewer via CODEOWNERS — include"`: `userReq("alice", true)` only → wantCount: 1
- `"CODEOWNERS + other reviewer — exclude"`: `userReq("alice", true)` + `userReq("bob", false)` → wantCount: 0
- `"explicit + CODEOWNERS on same user — include"`: `userReq("alice", false)` + `userReq("alice", true)` → wantCount: 1

Remove or update:
- Cases that test `SharesTeamWith` behaviour (no longer part of filterDirect logic)

## TestFilterCodeowner changes

Flipping semantics on two existing cases:
- `"sole codeowner"`: `userReq("alice", true)` only → **wantCount: 0** (was 1) — sole reviewer → direct mode now
- `"team codeowner, user is member"`: `teamReq("backend", true)` → **wantCount: 0** (was 1) — team request → team mode now

Add:
- `"CODEOWNERS + other reviewer — include (fallback)"`: `userReq("alice", true)` + `userReq("bob", false)` → wantCount: 1
- `"CODEOWNERS + team request — exclude (team wins)"`: `userReq("alice", true)` + `teamReq("backend", false)` → wantCount: 0

## TestFilterTeam changes

Add at minimum one case with `asCodeOwner: true` on the team request to document that filterTeam ignores AsCodeOwner:
- `"team request via CODEOWNERS — include"`: `teamReq("backend", true)` → wantCount: 1

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 3 TestFilter* suites pass with the new implementation
- make test passes
- No test relies on removed helpers (classifyReviewRequests, allTrue)

title: Implement disk-based team member caching
description:
## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Implement a `DiskCacher` type in `internal/cache/disk.go` that satisfies the `github.Cacher` interface
- [ ] Cache directory defaults to `~/.cache/gh-inbox/`; configurable via constructor
- [ ] Each entry stored as a file keyed by a URL-safe hash of the cache key
- [ ] TTL-based expiry (default 1 hour): check `mtime` on read; stale entries treated as cache miss
- [ ] Wire `DiskCacher` into `github.Client` in `cmd/prs/review.go` via a constructor option or factory
- [ ] Tests cover: cache miss (file absent), cache hit (file fresh), stale entry (file old), write failure (graceful fallback)

close_reason: All acceptance criteria met: scaffold created, dummy JSON output, tests pass

title: V1.0: Config file, multiple orgs, polish
Rewrite filterDirect and filterCodeowner with new semantics
Redefine filter modes for prs review
description:
## Summary

Redefine the --filter flag on prs review. The current modes (direct, codeowner) pivot on asCodeOwner. The new semantics redefine direct around team overlap and add a team mode.

| Mode | New behavior |
|------|-------------|
| direct | I'm requested as a User AND no other User reviewer shares any team with me |
| codeowner | All my review requests have asCodeOwner=true (unchanged) |
| team | My team is requested AND no member of that team is individually tagged as a User reviewer |

## Changes

1. Add UserTeam type — internal/github/types.go
2. Add FetchMyTeams() — internal/github/team_members.go
3. Extend TeamService — internal/service/team.go (add SharesTeamWith)
4. Move filter logic to internal/service/filter.go — delete internal/filter/
5. Rewrite tests — internal/service/filter_test.go
6. Wire up CLI — cmd/prs/review.go
7. Update tests — cmd/prs/review_test.go, internal/service/team_test.go
8. Update docs — DESIGN.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- make test passes
- make lint passes
- make build passes
- All three filter modes work: direct, codeowner, team
- direct mode: includes PRs where I'm requested as User AND no other User reviewer shares a team with me
- team mode: includes PRs where my team is requested AND no User reviewer is a member of that team
- codeowner mode: includes PRs where all my review requests have asCodeOwner=true
- internal/filter/ package is deleted
close_reason: All filter modes implemented, tests pass, build clean
